programme.title: A Bite to Eat with Alice
programme.desc: Commonwealth Games gold-medal-winning netballer Jo Weston drops by and she and Alice turn a fruity dessert on its head as they get baking in the kitchen. Alice surprises her with a nutty treat, as Jo is nuts over nuts!

Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Jacob Greber.

programme.title: News Breakfast
programme.desc: James Glenday and Emma Rebellato are in Perth with the latest news, overnight developments, interviews and new details on the top stories this morning. Plus Catherine Murphy with sport and Nate Byrne with weather in studio.

programme.title: ABC News Mornings
programme.desc: Stay in the know with what's happening this morning. ABC News brings you up-to-the-minute news to fuel your daily conversations so you can stay connected and follow the discussion across the day. Presented by Lorna Dunkley.

programme.title: Compass
programme.desc: Filmmaker Patrick Abboud meets Palestinian journalists who risked everything to document Gaza. Now safe in Australia, they reveal extraordinary evidence of continued killing of journalists.

programme.title: Stuff the British Stole
programme.desc: For well over a century the remains of Yagan, an Indigenous Noongar warrior from WA, were missing in the UK. The search for Yagan and the fight to bring him home is one of history's wildest detective stories.

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: The team tackle rock music memorabilia market. Can they prove a silver guitar once belonged to Ronnie Wood of the Rolling Stones?

programme.title: ABC News at Noon
programme.desc: Ros Childs presents the leading news at lunchtime, including the day's top stories and the latest news as it unfolds, with reporters in the community across Australia and around the world reporting on the issues that matter.

programme.title: The Piano
programme.desc: At Melbourne's Dandenong Market, people from all walks of life take a seat at the piano, sharing their stories and musical passion while master musicians Guy Sebastian and Andrea Lam observe.

programme.title: Can You Keep A Secret?
programme.desc: Debbie takes pity on blackmailer Marcus and invites him for lunch. She discovers that he and Pamela aren't in cahoots after all. The Fendons had two separate blackmail threats, and Pam is still at large and wants her money!

programme.title: The Assembly
programme.desc: Hosted by veteran ABC journalist, Leigh Sales, this week The Assembly sees the Autistic journalism students interview Australian music royalty, Delta Goodrem.

programme.title: Grand Designs Transformations
programme.desc: A little Melbourne workers' cottage gets a radical reimagining as a mid-century Palm Springs oasis, while across town, a backyard shed is transformed into a Japanese bathhouse.

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: A savage murder seemingly at the jaws of a wild beast leads Murdoch into North American lycanthropy. Searching for a rational explanation, Murdoch must choose between Enid and Dr. Ogden.

programme.title: Long Lost Family: What Happened Next
programme.desc: Davina and Nicky revisit Susan who, after having Sarah, had moved to South Africa in the 1970s. An update on Jeannie, who met her brother's adoptive sibling Robert, and Stella, who was reunited with daughter Maxine.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team continue their tour in search of treasures. They disembark at the Steam Museum in Swindon where objects attracting the expert eye include one of the rarest pieces of silver even seen on the show.

programme.title: Hard Quiz
programme.desc: Hard Quiz puts four self-nominated experts under Tom Gleeson's merciless microscope. This week's topics are Serena Williams, Sidney Nolan, Harley-Davidson motorcycles, and '90s movie Clueless!

programme.title: ABC News
programme.desc: News from where we live. ABC News NSW brings you today's top stories, exclusive investigations and original reporting on issues that matter. Presented by Lydia Feng, sport with Jared Coote and weather with Tom Saunders.

programme.title: Gardening Australia
programme.sub-title: Autumn: Grow Cacti, Lychees & Organic Veg
programme.desc: Costa explores a vibrant autumn garden, Guest Presenter Thanh gets stuck into lychees, Sophie has natives for formal styles, Jerry finds a huge cacti collection, and we meet radio presenter and veggie gardener Phil Dudman.

programme.title: Patience
programme.desc: The father of an autistic girl is killed in front of her and her mother goes missing. Patience connects with the girl and helps uncover the truth.

programme.title: Tonight At The Museum
programme.desc: An after-hours adventure at the museum as Alex Lee welcomes Pete Helliar, Danielle Walker, Alex Ward and Briggs for laughs and exhibition glory.

programme.title: The Weekly with Charlie Pickering
programme.desc: The news has never been more of a mess, but Charlie Pickering tries to make sense of the nonsense. We watch the week so that you don't have to. With special guests Rhys Nicholson, Nicolette Minster and Felicity Ward.

programme.title: ABC Late News
programme.desc: End your day with the latest on the day's top stories. ABC News has reporters across Australia and around the globe to uncover what's happening and why it matters. Presented by Craig Smart.

programme.title: Shaun Micallef's Eve of Destruction
programme.desc: Another hilarious episode of Shaun Micallef's Eve of Destruction, with celebrity guests Rhys Nicholson and Marcia Hines.

programme.title: Murdoch Mysteries
programme.desc: A savage murder seemingly at the jaws of a wild beast leads Murdoch into North American lycanthropy. Searching for a rational explanation, Murdoch must choose between Enid and Dr. Ogden.

programme.title: Love Me
programme.desc: It's Christine's funeral. Glen, Clara and Aaron have very different emotional responses to their grief which propels them each on a new path.

programme.title: rage
programme.desc: Your weekend begins with an all-night music video marathon. rage brings you New Releases showcasing the latest in buzzed-about and boundary-pushing music videos.

programme.title: rage
programme.desc: Keep the party vibe going as the sun comes up with continuous music videos, from new releases to classics and the songs we've long forgotten.

programme.title: rage
programme.desc: Counts down the hottest music videos of the week, with the latest chart-toppers and brand-new hit predictions.

programme.title: Weekend Breakfast
programme.desc: All the news you need to start your day. Fauziah Ibrahim and Johanna Nicholson bring you the latest news, interviews, politics, culture, weather and global affairs. Plus sport with Daniela Intili.

programme.title: rage
programme.desc: Keep the party vibe going with wall-to-wall music videos, from classic hits and long-forgotten tracks from the vault, to the latest new releases and special features.

programme.title: rage
programme.desc: Australia's longest running music TV show is home to the biggest vault of music videos from a diverse range of artists, genres and eras. Guest programmers curate a playlist of their all-time favourite clips.

programme.title: ABC News at Noon
programme.desc: Lorna Dunkley presents the latest from ABC News on the weekend bringing you today's top stories and coverage of events as they unfold. Plus original reporting, analysis and updated news, sport, weather and information.

programme.title: Blue Murder Motel
programme.desc: Local councillor Gordon has been stabbed to death in his home, with his cat - the only witness. Vinny and Cole's investigation is thwarted by the arrival of a homicide detective from the city.

programme.title: Patience
programme.desc: The father of an autistic girl is killed in front of her and her mother goes missing. Patience connects with the girl and helps uncover the truth.

programme.title: Whale Wisdom
programme.desc: A revealing documentary that uncovers the profound mystery of whales. Their environment's different, their senses are different, their relationships are different. How might such almost alien creatures see the world?

programme.title: Shaun Tan's Tales from Outer Suburbia
programme.desc: Klara and Pim go with Grandma and Grandpa on a thrilling treasure hunt, beyond the borders of suburbia. Faced with perplexing challenges, can they stick together and find their way home?

programme.title: Shaun Tan's Tales from Outer Suburbia
programme.desc: Klara's self-doubt threatens to swallow her whole, while Pim's birthday takes a strange turn when a dugong inexplicably appears on the front lawn, far from the ocean and in desperate need of help.

programme.title: Shaun Tan's Tales from Outer Suburbia
programme.desc: Klara nervously heads to the city for her art competition with supportive Pim. When the journey doesn't go as planned, her despair leads to a confrontation that will change her and her neighbourhood forever.

programme.title: Judgment: Cases That Changed Australia
programme.desc: Two men walk into Hobart police station, confess to a crime and begin High Court battles for justice, respect and marriage equality.

programme.title: Australian Story
programme.desc: Belgian backpacker Celine Cremer was on the trip of a lifetime but on a hike in Tasmania she took a wrong turn and was never seen again. Friends and volunteers set out looking for answers.

programme.title: Landline
programme.desc: A tasty sampler of stories from the land, covering food, agriculture, crops, climate, business and innovation, and meeting the fascinating people who make rural and regional communities unique. Presented by Pip Courtney.

programme.title: Kath & Kim
programme.desc: Brett is working 48 hours straight and a bored Kim makes a nuisance of herself at Kath's. Sharon is reunited with old school friend Lisa-Marie, making Kim jealous.

programme.title: Tonight At The Museum
programme.sub-title: Who Wants to be a Millipede
programme.desc: The museum is closed... but Alex Lee is just getting started, joined by Alan Davies, Tegan Higginbotham, Zoe Coombs Marr and Brett Blake.

programme.title: ABC News
programme.desc: News from where we live. ABC News NSW brings you the day's top stories, exclusive investigations and original reporting on the issues that matter to you. Presented by Lydia Feng, plus sport with Jared Coote.

programme.title: Blue Murder Motel
programme.desc: Mowai Bay is in the path of a ferocious summer storm, forcing stranded holidaymakers to bunk down in the Blue Motel conference room. Vinny and Cole investigate the death of a local radio DJ.

programme.title: Top End Bub
programme.desc: With Ned living at Leroy's, Lauren and Ned have never felt more distant, and Taya struggles with life without Ned. Trevor gets a makeover and Daffy unveils her song.

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The team attempt to investigate the mysterious death of a British tourist. On the surface, it looks like a clear-cut accident, but they realise nothing is quite what it seems in this case.

programme.title: Frauds
programme.desc: Sam enlists an old friend's help leaving Bert feeling betrayed. With the heist imminent, Bert and Sam struggle to keep their secrets hidden and their partnership intact.

programme.title: rage
programme.desc: Australia's longest-running music show is home to the biggest vault of music videos from a diverse range of artists, genres, and eras. They curate the best clips of all-time to entertain for hours.
